chunks.text: EECS Faculty and Alumni recognized with UC Berkeley Achievement Awards - EECS at Berkeley Each year, UC Berkeley honors exceptional members of its community through the Achievement Awards. These awards recognize industry-shaping leaders, devoted alumni who have deeply impacted the campus, and young graduates whose early accomplishments show great promise. The awards are co-presented by the UC Berkeley Foundation and the Cal Alumni Association. This year, four of the seven recipients are members of the EECS community. Fiat Lux Faculty Award EECS Professor Ion Stoica received the Fiat Lux Faculty Award for his leadership and dedication to UC Berkeley’s mission. The award honors “a faculty member whose extraordinary contributions go above and beyond the call of duty to advance the university’s philanthropic mission and transform its research, teaching, and programs.” Campanile Excellence in Achievement Award Meng-Hsiung Kiang, M.S. ’92, Ph.D. ’97 and Valerie E. Taylor, Ph.D. ’91 were both awarded the Campanile Excellence in Achievement Award, which honors alumni whose outstanding professional accomplishments exemplify the excellence of a UC Berkeley education. Mark Bingham Award for Excellence in Achievement by Young Alumni John Schulman, Ph.D. ’16 was awarded the Mark Bingham Award for Excellence in Achievement by Young Alumni for his early career success.
